questions.question: What do we say when we want something?
questions.explanation: The text says: 'When we want something, we say please.'

questions.question: What do we say when someone helps us?
questions.explanation: The text says: 'When someone helps us, we say thank you.'

questions.question: It is polite to grab things from others.
questions.explanation: The text says: 'It is rude to grab things from others. We should ask first!'

questions.question: What should you do before entering a room?
questions.explanation: The text says: 'we knock before entering.'

questions.question: Which is more polite?
questions.explanation: The text says: 'May I...? sounds more formal' and is more polite.

questions.question: We should put away our phone when someone is talking to us.
questions.explanation: The text says: 'We should put away our phone when someone is talking to us.'

questions.question: Match each technology device with what it does.
Classify these words: good manners or bad manners?
'_____ I borrow your pen, please?' Which word fits?
questions.explanation: We use 'Can I...?' to ask for permission: 'Can I borrow your pen?'

questions.question: '_____ I use the computer?' Which word makes this very polite?
questions.explanation: 'May I...?' is the most polite way to ask for permission.

questions.question: Complete with 'Can' or 'May'.
questions.sentence: '_____ I have some water, please?' '_____ I go to the library?' asked the student politely.

questions.question: 'Can I...?' and 'May I...?' both ask for permission.
questions.explanation: Both 'Can I...?' and 'May I...?' ask for permission. 'May I...?' is more formal.

questions.question: Which sentence asks for permission correctly?
questions.explanation: The correct way to ask is: 'Can I use the phone?'

questions.question: Put the words in order to make a polite question.
Put the words in order to make a polite question.
Match the polite word with when to use it.
What does 'thoughtful' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Thoughtful' means being kind and thinking about how to help others.

questions.question: What does 'rude' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Rude' means not being polite or respectful to others.

questions.question: Classify these technology devices.
Complete with the correct polite words.
'You _____ use my computer.' (giving permission) Which word fits?
questions.explanation: To give permission, we say 'You can...': 'You can use my computer.'

questions.question: We should ask before taking a photo of someone.
questions.explanation: The text says: 'we should always ask: Can I take your photo?'

questions.question: Complete with the correct word.
questions.sentence: We should _____ our work before playing. We should _____ devices with others.

questions.question: Match the vocabulary word with its meaning.
Which is a polite way to ask for water?
options: Can I have some water, please?
questions.explanation: 'Can I have some water, please?' uses both 'Can I' and 'please'.

questions.question: Put the words in order to make a sentence.
'Can I...?' is ONLY used to ask about ability, never for permission.
questions.explanation: 'Can I...?' is used both for ability AND permission: 'Can I go to the park?'

questions.question: Complete the polite sentences.
questions.sentence: _____ I take your photo? Yes, you _____!
